answer:
The key is to compare the cost-effectiveness of heat transfer per unit volume. The performance factor can be approximated by conductivity divided by density times cost. For aluminium, this is \(78 / (2400 \times 2) \approx 0.01625\), and for copper, \(190 / (8400 \times 1) \approx 0.0226\), making copper more economical.  

\boxed{C}
